perturbed_task_language: Put the cream cheese on the bowl
perturbation_semantics: objects/fixtures relocated; bowl ended up at world (-0.18, -0.04, top z=0.95), cream cheese at (-0.388, +0.206, z=0.92)
expected_baseline_behavior: Pi0 fullshot tries default trajectory and likely fails because bowl is not at training position
strategy_notes: Two-camera localization. Agentview at step 0: cream cheese identified by blue color (centroid pixel (98,179)) → world (-0.388, 0.206, top z=0.921). Initial confusion: agentview hot pixels at upper-mid (rows 65-85, cols 115-145) misleadingly suggested a far 'silver bowl' at world (-0.60, 0.005), but those pixels were actually the gripper itself viewed from agentview (eef home at world (-0.21, 0, 1.17) projected to that area). Real akita_black_bowl_1 is in agentview pixels around (128-140, 105-130), world (-0.18, -0.04, top z=0.95) — was OCCLUDED by the gripper at step 0. Discovered via wrist cam at step 6 (eef carried cream cheese to (-0.01, 0.11)) — wrist showed only stove burner + plate, no bowl, so I moved back. At step 9 wrist (eef -0.24, 0.05) saw silver-rimmed bowl with food in upper-right at world (~-0.16, -0.05). Confirmed via forward projection of world (-0.16, -0.05, 0.95) → agentview pixel (128, 113), matching the dark-grey ring at rows 120-140 cols 105-125 in step 0 agentview, which I had originally dismissed as table+wine-bottle. Stove burner (concentric rings at world (+0.13, +0.13)) and stove knob ('mushroom' shape) were the look-alikes I almost released onto first. Final: cream cheese box released at eef (-0.18, -0.04, z=0.99) — predicate fired.
